title: Forge v2 now owns the high-fidelity document translation change_request mainline
description: The active `.forge/` ledger no longer points at runtime self-heal closure as the current mainline. It now treats high-fidelity document translation foundation as the authoritative change_request path.
verification: Read .forge/spec/SPEC.md and confirm the north star is high-fidelity document translation foundation
Read .forge/DECISIONS.md and confirm the mainline decision has been rewritten accordingly
Read .forge/STATE.md and confirm batch-64 is the active change_request slice

title: Document parse revisions persist canonical IR sidecar truth
description: The system can persist parse revisions and revision artifacts so canonical IR exists as durable file truth rather than ephemeral parser output.
verification: Create parse revision ORM models and migration
Persist a parse revision plus canonical IR artifact record
Confirm the stored artifact path points at a materialized canonical IR sidecar

title: Canonical IR has a minimal executable schema
description: Canonical IR defines a stable minimal schema for document, chapter, block, asset, and relation truth plus JSON serialization helpers.
verification: Instantiate canonical IR types from a representative ParsedDocument
Serialize the canonical IR to JSON
Reload the same JSON into canonical IR objects without losing core fields

title: Execution artifacts carry parse revision and canonical node provenance
description: Projected Block and Sentence artifacts can point back to parse revision and canonical node identity without breaking the current bootstrap flow.
verification: Run ParseService on a representative ParsedDocument
Confirm Block provenance includes parse_revision_id and canonical_node_id
Confirm Sentence provenance carries the same upstream identity through segmentation

title: Canonical IR foundation is covered by targeted tests and compile checks
description: The first slice is not considered complete until targeted tests and compile checks prove the new parse revision skeleton works and does not obviously regress the repo.
verification: Run the targeted canonical IR unittest slice
Run py_compile across the new parse revision and canonical IR files
Confirm the baseline init smoke still passes or remains intentionally unchanged

title: EPUB export gains a source-preserving patch path
description: The system can patch original EPUB spine/DOM structure instead of relying only on rebuilt EPUB output.
verification: Introduce a source-preserving EPUB patch exporter
Preserve TOC, internal anchors, and footnote links
Verify translated text is written back without rebuilding the entire book

title: PDF planning persists page and zone extraction intent
description: The parser can record page-level or zone-level extraction modes such as native_text, ocr_overlay, and hybrid_merge as part of canonical truth.
verification: Introduce PDF page or zone planning metadata
Persist extraction mode and risk reasons into canonical truth
Confirm mixed-risk PDFs no longer depend only on document-level routing semantics

title: Translation units project from canonical truth rather than raw block heuristics
description: The translation orchestration layer can derive translation units from canonical nodes instead of only the current coarse block grouping.
verification: Introduce TranslationUnit projection objects
Project representative body/caption/reference units from canonical IR
Confirm packet construction can consume projected unit provenance

title: Canonical-truth export gates and source maps exist
description: Exporter-grade source maps and parse-projection validation gates exist so canonical truth can be verified before and after export.
verification: Introduce export source-map sidecar output
Add parse-projection validation or gate checks
Confirm failures can be surfaced as structured issues instead of silent drift
